content: Threat intel report: A new threat actor named Fancy Bear has been observed targeting the manufacturing sector using a custom malware dropper called DROPBEAR.
Incident ticket: Server ALPHA in the manufacturing plant was compromised. We found traces of DROPBEAR.
Security bulletin: Server ALPHA has been successfully patched and the malware was eradicated. Server ALPHA is now considered secure.
Industry report: Cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike has formally attributed the DROPBEAR campaigns to APT28. They note that Fancy Bear and APT28 are aliases for the same Russian GRU unit.
Vulnerability scan: Detected CVE-2024-1111 on the main firewall. This is a critical remote code execution vulnerability.
Patch deployment: The firewall vulnerability CVE-2024-1111 has been patched system-wide.
Threat briefing: APT28 has shifted tactics. They are no longer using DROPBEAR and have moved exclusively to exploiting edge devices using compromised credentials.
